articles.title: BUILD SECURE AI AGENTS WITHOUT COMPROMISING INNOVATION
articles.summary: AI agents are reshaping digital experiences, but securing them requires rethinking identity and access controls built for a human-first world. That's why we built Auth0 for AI Agents [4], now GA.

articles.title: 2026 MAY BE THE YEAR OF THE MEGA IPO
articles.summary: At least three of the most valuable and highest-profile tech companies are currently preparing to list their shares on the public market. Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX have all taken early steps to go public. Any of these companies would be among the most valuable ever to go public. The listings could create an enormous bonanza for Wall Street, which has seen a slump in IPOs since 2021.

articles.title: MUSK SAYS TESLA IS MOVING FULL SELF-DRIVING TO A MONTHLY SUBSCRIPTION
articles.summary: Tesla will stop selling its Full Self-Driving (Supervised) software for a flat rate and instead only offer it as a monthly subscription after February 14. The feature will start at $99 per month. It was previously priced at a one-time payment of $8,000. The technology still requires a human driver at the wheel ready to steer or brake at any time.

articles.title: ELON MUSK SURPRISES EVERYONE: SPACEX WILL ATTEMPT TO REACH MARS BY THE END OF 2026
articles.summary: SpaceX has announced plans for a potential uncrewed mission to Mars as early as late 2026. The proposed schedule coincides with a rare planetary alignment between Earth and Mars that offers a narrow but valuable launch opportunity. Several core technologies essential to deep space travel remain in development. Starship Version 3 is expected to debut this year. It will feature upgrades that may prove pivotal to long-duration missions beyond Earth orbit.

articles.title: GENETIC THERAPY AIMS TO BRING HEARING TO THOSE BORN DEAF
articles.summary: OTOF-related hearing loss results from a genetic change in a single critical protein. The auditory nerve fails to receive the proper signals from the hair cells in the ear due to the lack of the proper protein, resulting in profound deafness. DB-OTO is a virus-based gene therapy that can deliver a working version of the OTOF gene. It uses a non-pathogenic virus to carry the proper genetic code that produces the protein. In an early trial of the DB-OTO treatment, eleven out of twelve patients developed improved hearing within weeks, and nine patients no longer required cochlear implants.

articles.title: FEEL LIKE YOU'RE WORKING MORE, BUT GETTING LESS DONE? INSIDE THE HIDDEN PRODUCTIVITY CRISIS
articles.summary: 55% of knowledge workers are putting in more hours but delivering less. The culprit? Well over half say that fragmentation is a productivity killer. Miro's 2025 Momentum at Work Report [9] lays out the costs of tool sprawl and the solutions that get teams back on

articles.title: SCALING LONG-RUNNING AUTONOMOUS CODING
articles.summary: Model choice matters for extremely long-running tasks. GPT-5.2 models perform better at extended autonomous work compared to Opus 4.5 and GPT-5.1-codex. Many improvements come from removing complexity rather than adding it. The best system is often simpler than you'd expect. The harness and models matter, but the prompts matter more.

articles.title: JUNIOR DEVELOPERS IN THE AGE OF AI
articles.summary: The most compelling reason to hire juniors is AI itself. The biggest barrier to AI transformation has been workforce buy-in. Gen Z leads in AI adoption, and they act as an accelerant, helping colleagues learn AI tools and workflows. AI has lowered the cost of onboarding. If the infrastructure already exists, hiring juniors may be one of the highest-leverage AI bets available today.

articles.title: 2026: THIS IS AGI
articles.summary: Long-horizon agents allow models to take actions and iterate over time. The rate of progress in this area of research is exponential. It is estimated that agents will be able to work reliably to complete tasks comparably to human experts by 2028. People will soon be able to hire agents - one of the litmus tests for AGI.

articles.title: WHEN WILL THEY TAKE OUR JOBS?
articles.summary: Humans will lose control over the future if AI capabilities continue to advance. However, if we manage to avoid that, there's a good chance humans will retain a lot of employment during the Cyborg Era, which might last around 10 to 20 years. It is implausible that AI capabilities and available compute continue to increase indefinitely. This is a transitional state.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC IS MAKING A HUGE MISTAKE
articles.summary: Anthropic blocking third parties from using the Claude Code API will not convert people back to Claude Code, it will just convert people to other model providers.

articles.title: GAS TOWN DECODED
articles.summary: This post presents a list of terms used in Gas Town, an AI agent orchestration tool, decoding the definitions used in the introductory paper for developers.

articles.title: GOOGLE LAUNCHES PERSONAL INTELLIGENCE FEATURE IN GEMINI APP, CHALLENGING APPLE INTELLIGENCE
articles.summary: Google is testing a new AI tool with users that connects information from various apps to provide personalized answers in its chatbot.

articles.title: GETTING REAL LEVERAGE FROM CLAUDE CODE
articles.summary: This post discusses how to run multiple Claude Code sessions in parallel, each in its own fully isolated environment.

articles.title: AI ENABLES AS-NEEDED SOFTWARE FEATURES
articles.summary: AI enables people to just describe what they want, and then apps write the code needed to do it on demand.

articles.title: DIGG AND REDDIT CREATORS RELAUNCH DIGG TO COMPETE WITH REDDIT
articles.summary: Digg has relaunched with a focus on trust signals, transparency in moderation, and defenses against AI-driven spam.
